data.title: Revolve Group Price Target Maintained With a $30.00/Share by BTIG
Nokia Plans to Reposition in Move to Focus on AI Market
data.description:
(11:02 GMT) *Nokia Now Targets to Grow Annual Comparable Op Pft to EUR2.7B-EUR3.2B by 2028
(11:04 GMT) *Nokia: New Strategy Will Focus on AI and Cloud, AI-Native Networks and 6G
(11:05 GMT) *Nokia Will Co-Innovate With Customers and Partners, Put Capital Where It Can Differentiate, Unlock Sustainable Returns
(11:07 GMT) *Nokia: Mobile Networks Head Tommi Uitto to Step Down From Group Leadership Team From Dec. 31
(11:09 GMT) *Nokia To Assess Future of Several Businesses Now Seen as Non-Core
(11:11 GMT) *Nokia Defense Launched as a Dedicated Unit
(11:13 GMT) *Nokia Targets 6%-8% Compound Annual Growth in Network Infrastructure Sales During 2025-2028
(11:14 GMT) *Nokia Targets Growth in Mobile Infrastructure Operating Profit From a Base of EUR1.5B
(11:39 GMT) Nokia Plans to Reposition in Move to Focus on AI Market

By Dominic Chopping

Nokia outlined plans to reposition itself and focus on the burgeoning artificial-intelligence market by simplifying its operational model and shuffling its leadership team.

The Finnish telecom-equipment provider announced a new strategy that will see it work to accelerate growth in AI and cloud while developing new technology that incorporates AI into the next generation of mobile networks.

The plans were announced ahead of an investor day where Chief Executive Justin Hotard will present the new strategy, long-term financial targets, and changes to the leadership team.

The focus on AI comes after Nvidia recently invested $1 billion in the company, taking a 2.9% stake through the acquisition of newly-issued shares.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: ExxonMobil to Close Scottish Chemicals Plant
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

ExxonMobil plans to close its Fife ethylene plant in Scotland next year, a move that affects more than 400 jobs.

The U.S. energy major said it had considered various options to continue operations, including looking for a potential buyer, but concluded that the plant isn't currently commercially viable.

The company said the U.K.'s economic and policy environment, combined with market conditions, high costs and plant efficiency challenges all contributed to the decision.

There are 179 direct employees at risk, as well as 250 contractors. ExxonMobil said there are roughly 50 opportunities for staff from the site to move to its Fawley refinery in the South of England.

The Fife plant produces ethylene which is used in plastics, medical equipment, and other everyday products.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: UK – State funds into Pentland offshore floater
data.description:
By Rod Morrison

Great British Energy, National Wealth Fund and Scottish National Investment Bank have taken an option to invest up to £50m each in the 100MW Pentland floating offshore wind scheme being developed by Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners via CI 1V Highland HoldCo 2.

The scheme is expected to have been bid into last week's AR7 contract for difference tender. In June last year it was announced Japanese developer Eurus Energy had become a part-owner in the scheme alongside CIP and Hexicon. The latest release does not mention Eurus.

data.title: LUXE: Mytheresa drove strong growth amid group transformation, with improved margins and segment realignment
data.description:
Q1 FY26 saw Mytheresa deliver double-digit sales and profit growth, while group GMV and Net Sales declined moderately but gross margins improved. The transformation plan is on track, with THE OUTNET divestment and segment realignment supporting future efficiency.

Original document: LuxExperience B.V. Sponsored ADR [LUXE] SEC 6-K Current Report — Nov. 19 2025

data.title: Memory chip makers rise; report says Nvidia move may double prices
data.description:
** Shares of memory chip makers rise premarket

** Seagate Technology up 0.72%; Micron Technology adds 2.8%

** SanDisk rises 1.87%; Western Digital advances 1.06%

** Report from Counterpoint Research says Nvidia’s switch to smartphone-style LPDDR memory for AI servers could double server-memory prices by late 2026

** Low-power Double Data Rate (LPDDR) is a type of computer memory designed to use less power than standard DDR (Double Data Rate) memory

** Counterpoint says NVDA's move to LPDDR to cut power costs will create sudden demand the industry cannot easily absorb

** Each AI server needs far more LPDDR chips than a handset, making Nvidia a customer on the scale of a major smartphone maker — a “seismic shift” for supply chains

** Counterpoint expects server-memory prices to double by end-2026, raising costs for cloud providers and AI developers

data.title: TSX futures rise on commodity boost as investors await Nvidia results
data.description:
Futures tied to Canada's main stock index rose on Wednesday, supported by higher gold prices, while investor attention centered on Nvidia's quarterly earnings due after market close.

Futures on the S&P/TSX composite index (SXFcv1) rose 0.3% as of 6:00 a.m. ET, signaling a higher open.

The benchmark index fell for the second straight session on Tuesday amid mounting doubts about the stretched valuations in tech and artificial intelligence stocks as well as ambitious spending on the sector.

All eyes were on Nvidia's third-quarter results, which could either extend or stall the tone for AI-driven market rally that has been responsible for most gains on Wall Street this year.

Investors are also watching a delayed U.S. labor report, now scheduled for Thursday, which could confirm weakness in the job market and reignite hopes of a Federal Reserve rate cut.

Gold prices rose by 1%, helped by higher demand for safe-haven assets as investors awaited the release of delayed U.S. jobs report, alongside Wednesday's Fed minutes from its most recent meeting.

Copper prices snapped a three-day streak of declines.

Meanwhile, Bank of Canada Deputy Governor Nicolas Vincent is set to speak later in the day, potentially offering clues on the country's policy path.

In corporate news, JPMorgan raised the price target on business jet maker Bombardier to C$215 from C$170.

Cenovus Energy announced a $1.6 billion offering of senior notes.

data.title: Global liquor giants seek $337 million overdue payment from Indian state
data.description:
By Aditya Kalra

Alcohol industry groups representing Heineken, Diageo and Pernod Ricard have urged the southern Indian state of Telangana to settle $337 million in overdue payments, saying further delay risks supply shortages and threatens business continuity, a letter seen by Reuters shows.

Telangana is one of the largest alcohol-consuming regions in the country but has had a tense relationship with beer and liquor makers over payments for supplies to government depots. In January, Heineken's United Breweries - which makes the popular Kingfisher beer brand - briefly halted supplies to the state in protest.

The pending dues, dating back to May 2024, add financial strain on the industry ahead of the festive season when demand typically rises by 75%, according to the letter sent to the state's government on November 12.

Signed by the Brewers Association of India, Confederation of Indian Alcoholic Beverage Companies and International Spirits and Wines Association of India, the letter urged the state to release the 29.85 billion rupees ($337 million).

"Non-clearance of old payments and delay in current payments have created a situation which is not sustainable for the industry," the private letter said.

"Many companies will not be able to continue operations as they do not have the financial resources to do so," the letter added, without specifying any company by name.

The Telangana government did not respond to repeated requests for comment from Reuters.

Heineken, Diageo and Pernod also did not respond to queries.

Many regions like Telangana require liquor companies to supply only to state-run depots, which then sell to retailers, forcing the companies to rely on state governments for payment.

The alcohol sector in India, a key growth market for global beer and liquor producers, is already contending with increasingly stringent regulations, including planned tougher advertising restrictions and antitrust investigations.

data.title: Nvidia and Microsoft Strike Massive AI Deal With Anthropic -- What It Means For Investors
data.description:
Microsoft , Nvidia and Anthropic outlined a major partnership Tuesday that includes strategic investments and a long-term compute pact.

Nvidia and Microsoft plan to invest up to $10 billion and up to $5 billion, respectively, while Anthropic agreed to commit roughly $30 billion of spending on Microsoft Azure and to contract additional compute capacity up to one gigawatt.

The investments value Anthropic at about $350 billion, according to media reports, up from a $183 billion valuation after a September $13 billion raise.

Anthropic's frontier Claude models, Claude Sonnet 4.5, Claude Opus 4.1 and Claude Haiku 4.5 will be available on Microsoft's Azure AI Foundry, making Claude accessible across the top three cloud platforms.

Nvidia and Anthropic will also work to optimize those models for future Nvidia architectures and to use Anthropic's Grace Blackwell and Vera Rubin systems within the compute commitment.

For investors, the deal signals deepening collaboration in AI and cloud infrastructure, potentially boosting growth prospects for MSFT and NVDA, though the large commitments also carry execution and valuation risks.

data.title: FHTX: SMARCA2 program advances with Lilly, while proprietary degraders near IND and partnerships are sought
data.description:
The conference highlighted advances in chromatin biology drug discovery, with a major SMARCA2 program in partnership with Eli Lilly progressing toward a key decision in 2026. data.title: Lowe's 3Q Sales Rise, Boosted by Growth in Pro Builder Business — Update
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

Lowe's reported higher third-quarter sales as continued growth in its business supplying professional builders helped it to offset weakness in the home improvement market.

The home improvement retailer reported net income of $1.62 billion, or $2.88 a share, down from $1.7 billion, or $2.99 a share, the year prior. The company incurred $129 million in pre-tax expenses associated with recent acquisitions.

Adjusted earnings were $3.06 a share. Analysts had expected $2.97, according to FactSet.

Sales rose to $20.81 billion, up from $20.17 billion the year before. Wall Street had expected $20.82 billion.

Comparable sales rose 0.4%, driven by 11% online sales growth, double-digit growth in home services and continued growth in its professional builder supply business. Analysts had expected 1% comparable sales growth.

The company raised its full-year sales outlook to $86 billion, up from its previous forecast of $84.5 billion to $85.5 billion. It guided for flat comparable sales relative to the prior year, compared with its previous guidance of flat to 1% growth.

It also forecast full-year adjusted earnings of $12.25 a share, compared with its previous guidance of $12.20 to $12.45 a share.

Analysts see full-year sales of $85.31 billion, comparable sales growth of 0.3% and adjusted earnings of $12.28 a share.

Home improvement activity has sagged as a stagnant housing market, high interest rates and general uncertainty about the economic environment led homeowners to delay remodeling and repair projects. On Tuesday, Home Depot trimmed its full-year outlook and said that an expected uptick in home improvement demand had failed to materialize.

Home improvement retailers have said that an aging housing stock and surging home equity values will eventually lead to a turnaround in home improvement demand. But so far, the market has shown few signs of picking up even as interest rates have crept lower. According to Placer.ai, foot traffic at Lowe's in the third quarter slipped 0.1% from the previous year.

But Home Depot and Lowe's have both expanded their businesses supplying professional builders. Last month, Lowe's completed its $8.8 billion acquisition of Foundation Building Materials, marking a significant leap into the professional builder market following large acquisitions by Home Depot. The two companies are competing for the more consistent spending of professional building and repair companies, particularly as consumers delay large remodels and do-it-yourself projects, analysts say.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com

data.title: Lineage, Inc. Announces Euro-Denominated Senior Notes Offering
data.description:
On November 19, 2025, Lineage, Inc. announced a private offering of euro-denominated senior notes.

Key Highlights:

  • Notes offered to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A.
  • Proceeds to repay revolving credit facility and for general purposes.
  • Notes guaranteed by Lineage and its subsidiaries, excluding the issuer.
  • Offering subject to market conditions and customary closing conditions.
  • Notes not registered under the Securities Act, limited to non-U.S. persons.

Original SEC Filing: Lineage, Inc. [ LINE ] - 8-K - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: Dollar Tree, Inc. to Host Q3 2025 Earnings Call
data.description:
On November 19, 2025, Dollar Tree, Inc. announced its upcoming Q3 2025 earnings call scheduled for December 3, 2025.

Key Highlights:

  • Q3 2025 results to be released before market opens on Dec 3.
  • CEO Mike Creedon and CFO Stewart Glendinning to lead the call.
  • Conference call set for 8 a.m. EST on Dec 3, 2025.
  • Webcast available on Dollar Tree's investor relations website.
  • Replay accessible for seven days post-call with passcode 13756945.
